visualGoal: Make a bind-drowning Star Citizen / Elite pilot understand that their voice is a free, unlimited input pool for ~$10 — and honestly frame the Stream Deck panel as the no-config hardware alternative. This is a low-product-density SOFTWARE how-to: no forced product cards. Visual weight should come from Codex infographics (cost ladder, decision tree, setup flow), not from padding hardware gearpicks. Only one genuinely relevant verified product image exists (Stream Deck XL) and it is used sparingly.

editorialCompositePrompts.prompt: Realistic dim home space-sim cockpit desk at night lit in cyan and magenta monitor glow, a headset boom mic in the foreground and an open desk area beside it left blank for a real Elgato Stream Deck XL product overlay, Star-Citizen-style starfield on the monitor (no real game UI, no copyrighted logos), the visual tension of 'talk to it OR press labeled keys', 16:9 editorial cover, no fake branded hardware, no fake game HUD.
editorialCompositePrompts.negativePrompt: fake logos, copyrighted game UI, warped keyboard, unreadable text, procedural gradient art, invented product hardware

infographicPrompts.title: What each layer costs and does
content: VoiceAttack v2 ~$10 (REQUIRED) — voice to keypress engine
Free community profile — prebuilt binds you adapt
HCS Voice Pack ~£10-23 (optional) — talking copilot personality
HCS starter bundle ~£26.99 — pack + sometimes VoiceAttack
Decent headset mic — the real make-or-break
WhisperAttack (free, NVIDIA GPU) — offline AI accuracy fix
infographicPrompts.style: IgnitionSim command-center stacked ladder, required tier highlighted, large readable text, space cyan/magenta palette, prices tagged 'approx / check current'

infographicPrompts.title: Voice, panel, or both?
content: Do you run out of stick buttons? No -> you need neither
Hate config / noisy room? -> Stream Deck XL (labeled hardware)
Want a talking copilot fantasy? -> VoiceAttack + HCS pack
Want max coverage + immersion? -> run BOTH (panel for deliberate, voice for conversational)
infographicPrompts.style: Clean branching decision tree, space palette, arrows, one clear endpoint per branch, no product photos required

infographicPrompts.title: The 6-step setup that avoids misfires
content: 1 Bind everything in-game to keyboard keys
2 Install VoiceAttack + TRAIN Windows Speech Recognition
3 Import a community profile, remap to your binds
5 Set push-to-talk or a wake keyword
infographicPrompts.style: Numbered horizontal flow, steps 2 and 4 flagged as 'skip these = misfires', space palette, large readable text

curatorScenePrompts.prompt: Realistic adult sim-hardware curator Val Chen at a tidy dual-monitor space-sim desk wearing a headset, one hand resting near a labeled macro panel, calm knowledgeable energy, cyan/magenta rim light, product-overlay space to one side, practical not fantasy, 4:5 portrait.

screenshotsOrGameMediaNeeded.need: A clean VoiceAttack profile-editor screenshot showing a phrase -> keypress command (e.g. 'deploy landing gear' -> a keybind), to illustrate the core mechanic in the 'What VoiceAttack actually does' section.
screenshotsOrGameMediaNeeded.sourceHint: Capture from VoiceAttack itself or use an official/press screenshot; verify licensing before embedding. Do NOT embed copyrighted game HUD.

openQuestions: Exact current USD price for HCS packs / ASTRA 2 starter bundle (store is GBP; FX + sales swing) before any hard dollar figure is quoted.
Current Elgato Stream Deck XL street price ($225 sale vs $250 MSRP) and MK.2 price at certification time.
WhisperAttack current minimum VRAM / release requirements — re-confirm on GitHub (fast-moving).
Is any current HCS pack attributable to a genuinely well-known celebrity/sci-fi actor? Not substantiated on pages checked — keep 'professional voice actors' unless verified.
Should we source a licensed VoiceAttack profile-editor screenshot to raise the visual count without forcing hardware cards?
